14th_circuit.name: 14th Circuit Court — Muskegon County
courtroom_rules: Arrive 30 minutes early — check in with clerk
Business attire (suit/tie preferred, minimum: dress shirt, slacks, dress shoes)
No cell phones in courtroom (turn OFF, not vibrate)
Stand when addressing the court
Address judge as "Your Honor" — even if seeking disqualification
Do NOT interrupt opposing counsel — note objections for your turn
Bring 3 copies of everything (court, opposing party, yourself)

federal.name: US District Court — Western District of Michigan
federal.address: 399 Federal Building, 110 Michigan St NW, Grand Rapids, MI 49503
courtroom_rules: Federal court is MORE formal than state court
Must register for CM/ECF (PACER) before filing
All filings through electronic system — no walk-in filings
Follow Federal Rules of Civil Procedure (FRCP)
Address judge as "Your Honor" or "Judge [Last Name]"
Arrive 45 minutes early for security screening
No electronics of any kind in courtroom
Stand when judge enters and exits

coa.name: Michigan Court of Appeals
coa.address: 925 W Ottawa St, Lansing, MI 48915 (Cadillac Place: 3020 W Grand Blvd, Detroit)
courtroom_rules: Oral argument is NOT guaranteed — granted only if court deems it helpful
If granted: strictly timed (usually 15-20 minutes per side)
Focus on legal arguments, NOT facts — judges have read the briefs
Be prepared for questions from the bench — they will interrupt
Know your record cites cold (page and line numbers)
Do NOT repeat what is in your brief — add value

objections_detail.when: Opposing party offers out-of-court statement for truth
objections_detail.how: "Objection, Your Honor — hearsay. The statement is offered for the truth of the matter asserted."

objections_detail.when: Evidence does not relate to any issue in the case
objections_detail.how: "Objection — relevance. This evidence does not tend to prove or disprove any fact of consequence."

objections_detail.when: Attorney asks leading questions on direct examination
objections_detail.how: "Objection — leading. Counsel is suggesting the answer to their own witness."

objections_detail.when: Document or evidence not properly authenticated
objections_detail.how: "Objection — lack of foundation. The exhibit has not been properly authenticated."

objections_detail.when: Witness testifies about something they have no personal knowledge of
objections_detail.how: "Objection — speculation. The witness has no personal knowledge of this matter."

objections_detail.when: Party offers copy when original is available
objections_detail.how: "Objection — best evidence rule. The original document should be produced."

objections_detail.when: Same point made repeatedly with multiple witnesses
objections_detail.how: "Objection — cumulative. This evidence is duplicative of what has already been presented."

objections_detail.when: Question presupposes something not yet proven
objections_detail.how: "Objection — the question assumes facts not in evidence."
